title: Aunt's Summer Visit
story_html: My aunt is cheerful and kind. She visits us every summer. Today, my sister and I feel excited. We wait for her at the door. When she arrives, we look surprised. She brings us fresh fruit. Fruit is nutritious for our bodies. We also eat some chips together. After the snack, I do my writing practice. My teacher gives different homework this week. I write about a mammal for science class.

nutritious.def: Full of things that are good for your body.

cheerful.def: Happy and full of good spirits.

surprised.def: Feeling shocked because something unexpected happens.

aunt.def: The sister of your mom or dad, or your uncle's wife.

different.def: Not the same as something else.

excited.def: Feeling very happy and full of energy about something.

chips.def: Thin, crispy slices of fried potato.

writing.def: Putting words on paper with a pen or pencil.

sister.def: A girl who has the same parents as you.

mammal.def: An animal that has fur and feeds milk to its babies.

questions.qtext: "My aunt is cheerful and kind." What does cheerful mean?
options: Happy and full of good spirits
questions.explain: Cheerful means happy and full of good spirits.

questions.qtext: "Fruit is nutritious for our bodies." What does nutritious mean?
questions.explain: Nutritious means full of things that are good for your body.

questions.qtext: Who visits the family every summer?
questions.explain: The story says, "My aunt is cheerful and kind. She visits us every summer."
The story says, "She brings us fresh fruit."

questions.qtext: What does the child write about for science class?
questions.explain: The story says, "I write about a mammal for science class."
